archetype: High-Frequency Gamma-Ray Burst Coincidence & Triangulation Engine for Multi-Messenger Astronomy
description: A distributed real-time processing system that ingests truncated telemetry streams from multiple independent satellite constellations to detect, filter, and triangulate transient gamma-ray bursts within sub-millisecond windows. It is used by the mission operations center to trigger rapid repointing of optical telescopes and issue automated public alerts, requiring nanosecond-precision clock synchronization across light-years of separation. The technical difficulty lies in correlating noisy, time-skewed data streams from disparate hardware architectures while adhering to strict latency budgets to capture the earliest optical afterglow.
rules: MUST implement sub-microsecond clock synchronization using PTP (IEEE 1588v2) or GPS disciplined oscillators to ensure valid triangulation geometry.
MUST process CCSDS (Consultative Committee for Space Data Systems) telemetry packets with zero-copy memory structures to prevent buffer overruns during burst modes.
MUST enforce deterministic latency for all signal path processing to guarantee a <100ms alert generation time post-detection.
MUST adhere to lossy compression standards specifically for high-energy photon counting data where statistical integrity must be preserved despite bandwidth constraints.
MUST integrate with the VOEvent protocol (IETF) for standardized inter-observatory alert broadcasting.
anti_patterns: DO NOT use standard time-series databases for raw photon arrival data; the write throughput during a swarm event will overwhelm disk I/O and break real-time processing.
DO NOT rely on TCP for telemetry ingestion; packets must be handled via UDP with application-layer sequence recovery to minimize jitter.
DO NOT implement batch processing for alert generation; the system must be event-driven and stream-based to meet the physics of the afterglow phenomenon.
key_technical_challenges: Resolving time-of-arrival ambiguities caused by relativistic effects and inter-satellite clock drift without a shared ground station anchor.
Filtering cosmic ray background noise from genuine astrophysical transient signals using real-time statistical hypothesis testing on embedded hardware.
Maintaining system integrity and data coherence when one node in the constellation experiences a single-event upset (SEU) due to radiation exposure.

recommended_stack.language: Rust + C++ (embedded); Rust for memory safety in high-concurrency alert logic, C++ for legacy hardware abstraction layers.
recommended_stack.framework: RustTokio + ZeroMQ; ensures asynchronous non-blocking I/O with deterministic latency guarantees required for time-critical signal processing.
recommended_stack.database_engine: TimescaleDB (with hypertables for time-series) + In-Memory Plasma; Timescale for historical trend analysis, Plasma for high-speed ring buffers.
recommended_stack.cache: Null (Direct Memory Access preferred); caching adds indeterminate latency which is unacceptable for transient detection.
recommended_stack.message_broker: ZeroMQ (ZMQ); provides the necessary pub/sub pattern with ultra-low latency and no single point of failure.

compliance_flags: ECSS (European Cooperation for Space Standardization) Standards
